calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE TERI L. JACKSON; Clerk EBura; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-19-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-23-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY, SANDRA L. RIBERA & ATTY. KRISTINA T. CARBONELL OF THE RIBERA LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 576-1600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRAD M. WEINTRAUB OF THE GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE GP, INC AND CHRISTIAN AGARDI'S Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In the Alternative, Summary Adjudication - The motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. The motion is denied as to the discrimination, retaliation, and wrongful discharge in violation of public policy causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant constructively discharged plaintiff. See Turner v. Anheuser Busch (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1238, 1244-1245; Plaintiff's deposition, 67:12-68:6). The trier of fact will decide whether the working conditions were so intolerable that a reasonable person in plaintiff's position would feel compelled to resign or whether plaintiff would have left the job notwithstanding the misconduct. Defendant is basically raising a factual issue regarding causation. The motion is denied as to the harassment and failure to correct harassment causes of action. The court looks at the totality of the circumstances to determine harassment. See Miller v. Dept. of Corrections (2005) 36 Cal.4th 446, 462. Whether defendant took immediate and appropriate corrective action is a factual issue. A trier of fact could find that defendant's investigations were half-hearted and defendant should have installed the video camera prior to the fifth graffiti incident. The trier of fact could also find that Mr. Agardi's conduct constitutes harassment. The motion is denied as to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2014 Set For Trial On Mar-23-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2014 Continued To Jul-30-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DANIEL DEAN, ESQ., 415.576.1600, FOR PLAINTIFF CRAIG HOLLIS.
TIMOTHY TRAVELSTEAD, ESQ., 415.986.5900, FOR DEFENDANTS UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE GP, INC., CHRISTIAN AGARDI, JEFF ELLIS AND MIKE LANCASTER.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS UNIVERSAL PROTECTION SERVICE GP, INC., CHRISTIAN AGARDI, JEFF ELLIS AND MIKE LANCASTER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT's second cause of action for Hostile Work Environment Harassment in Violation of FEHA as to all Defendants and sixth cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress as to individual Defendants Agardi, Ellis, and Lancaster is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to individual Defendants Ellis and Lancaster because the Complaint fails to allege that the conduct of these individual defendants was "severe or pervasive" or that their conduct fell outside the "realm of properly delegated personnel management authority." Janken v. GM Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 55, 79. The demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled as to individual Defendant Agardi and Defendant Universal Service Group, Inc. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action is overruled as to individual Defendant Agardi. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976
